title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a school trip.
extracts.audio_script: Boy: Are you going on the geography trip next month? || Woman: I hope so. The teacher said there are only a few places left. || Boy: I wanted to go, but my parents thought it was too expensive. Then the school changed the plan. [8]We're travelling by coach instead of train now, so the price is much lower[/8]. || Woman: That's good news. You should ask them again tonight. || Woman: I might ask someone else as well, just to be sure, but at least I have a much better idea now.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Are you going on the geography trip next month?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I hope so. The teacher said there are only a few places left.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I wanted to go, but my parents thought it was too expensive. Then the school changed the plan. We're travelling by coach instead of train now, so the price is much lower.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's good news. You should ask them again tonight.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I might ask someone else as well, just to be sure, but at least I have a much better idea now.

questions.question: Why might the boy be able to go on the trip?
questions.explanation: The boy says the trip is cheaper now because they are travelling by coach instead of train.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking to a friend about a new job.
extracts.audio_script: Man: How's your new job at the sports centre? || Woman: Busy, but good. I thought I'd spend all day answering phones, but I actually meet lots of customers. The manager is friendly, and the hours are okay. [9]The only thing I'm still learning is how to use the booking system on the computer[/9]. || Man: You'll get used to that soon. || Woman: Well, I hope it works out. It sounds as if you have thought about it carefully, which is always a sensible start.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: How's your new job at the sports centre?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Busy, but good. I thought I'd spend all day answering phones, but I actually meet lots of customers. The manager is friendly, and the hours are okay. The only thing I'm still learning is how to use the booking system on the computer.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: You'll get used to that soon.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Well, I hope it works out. It sounds as if you have thought about it carefully, which is always a sensible start.

questions.question: What does the woman say about her new job?
questions.explanation: She says she is still learning how to use the booking system on the computer.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about a birthday present.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you bought Dan's birthday present yet? || Man: Not yet. I nearly got him a football shirt, but I wasn't sure which team he supports now. || Woman: He keeps talking about cooking at the moment. [10]Why don't we get him that new recipe book he looked at last week?[/10] || Man: Great idea. It's easier than choosing clothes, and he'll actually use it. || Woman: Maybe next time it will be simpler. Things like this often seem more difficult before you know the full story.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you bought Dan's birthday present yet?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not yet. I nearly got him a football shirt, but I wasn't sure which team he supports now.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: He keeps talking about cooking at the moment. Why don't we get him that new recipe book he looked at last week?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Great idea. It's easier than choosing clothes, and he'll actually use it.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe next time it will be simpler. Things like this often seem more difficult before you know the full story.

questions.question: What present do they decide to buy?
questions.explanation: The woman suggests buying the recipe book Dan looked at, and the man agrees.

extracts.context: You will hear a man telling a friend about his weekend.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did you enjoy your weekend in York? || Man: Yes, though it rained most of Saturday. We couldn't walk along the river as planned, so we went to the old museum instead. [11]I didn't expect much, but the exhibition about trains was actually the highlight of the trip[/11]. || Woman: Sometimes the best things are the ones you don't plan. || Woman: I agree. It is worth taking a little time over it, instead of rushing and regretting the choice later.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did you enjoy your weekend in York?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, though it rained most of Saturday. We couldn't walk along the river as planned, so we went to the old museum instead. I didn't expect much, but the exhibition about trains was actually the highlight of the trip.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Sometimes the best things are the ones you don't plan.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I agree. It is worth taking a little time over it, instead of rushing and regretting the choice later.

questions.question: What did the man enjoy most?
questions.explanation: He says the train exhibition at the museum was the highlight of the trip.

extracts.context: You will hear two colleagues talking about a training day.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you going to the training day next Friday? || Woman: Yes, although I wasn't very interested at first. The morning talk sounds useful, but the afternoon workshop worries me a bit. [12]We have to practise speaking in front of the whole group, and I always get nervous doing that[/12]. || Man: I know what you mean, but it'll be good practice. || Woman: That makes sense. Sometimes the practical details are more important than they seem at the beginning.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you going to the training day next Friday?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, although I wasn't very interested at first. The morning talk sounds useful, but the afternoon workshop worries me a bit. We have to practise speaking in front of the whole group, and I always get nervous doing that.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I know what you mean, but it'll be good practice.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That makes sense. Sometimes the practical details are more important than they seem at the beginning.

questions.question: How does the woman feel about the afternoon workshop?
questions.explanation: She says she gets nervous speaking in front of a whole group.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a local park.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you seen the changes at the park? || Woman: Yes, and they're great. The new café is nice, although it gets crowded. The children's playground is bigger too. [13]What I really like is the new path around the lake, because I can now walk there even after heavy rain[/13]. || Man: That will be useful in winter. || Woman: I can understand that. It is always useful when someone explains the situation clearly before you decide what to do.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you seen the changes at the park?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and they're great. The new café is nice, although it gets crowded. The children's playground is bigger too. What I really like is the new path around the lake, because I can now walk there even after heavy rain.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That will be useful in winter.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I can understand that. It is always useful when someone explains the situation clearly before you decide what to do.

questions.question: What does the woman like most about the park?

options.A: the new path around the lake.

questions.explanation: She says what she really likes is the new path around the lake.
